description_long: This clock tower features a tall, slender black column rising from a square, tiered base, supporting a circular clock face set within an ornate, dark metallic housing. The top of the tower is adorned with a decorative, scalloped crest that adds a touch of historical elegance. The clock face is white with black markings and hands, encased in a round frame, and is the only visible face on the structure. The base appears solid and robust, likely made of stone or metal, providing stability to the tall, narrow shaft. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and neoclassical influences, suitable for placement in urban squares or parks. The materials and construction imply durability and resistance to outdoor elements.
description: A tall, ornate clock tower with a decorative crest, black column, and a single white clock face set in a round frame atop a sturdy square base.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the ornate crest and the back of the clock housing, with the column and base visible.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thinness of the column and the depth of the clock housing, with minimal detail on the face.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the white clock face with black markings, ornate crest, and the full structure from base to top.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the narrowness of the structure and the side of the clock housing.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular and white, featuring black Arabic numerals and minute markings around the perimeter. It has two black hands for hours and minutes, with no visible second hand. The design is simple and functional, with no additional decorative elements on the face itself.
